title: [WITHDRAWN] Functional States of T Cells in Scleroderma Peripheral Blood Reveal Distinct Cytokine Polarization Axes
author: Ritschel, Glen
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
WITHDRAWN (June 2026): The author withdraws the central claim of this manuscript. On reanalysis the finding that scleroderma peripheral-blood T cells occupy distinct cytokine-polarization functional states is not supported, for the reasons below. The data and code remain available for transparency; the biological and therapeutic conclusions should be treated as withdrawn. This manuscript was not accepted for publication. It is a standalone research paper and is not a companion to, and does not affect, any patent filing. 
 1. Pseudoreplication. Functional states were called at the cluster level and treated as independent observations, rather than aggregated per patient. The corrected per-patient analysis did not support the claimed state distinctions. 2. Cluster-level labeling. The "functional state" labels were assigned to Leiden clusters and then used to interpret the same clusters, without an external or clinical anchor. 3. Sample-structure issues. The analysis did not adequately account for donor/sample structure across the PBMC set, inflating apparent separation between states. 
 The withdrawal reflects responsible self-correction made on the author's own review. 
 --- ORIGINAL DESCRIPTION (claims WITHDRAWN, preserved for the record) --- 
 This archive contains the complete manuscript and supplementary materials for: 
 Ritschel, G.W. (2026). Single-cell transcriptomic analysis reveals program-based T cell functional states in systemic sclerosis peripheral blood. Frontiers in Immunology. Submitted. 
  CONTENTS 
 Manuscript: - Full manuscript (PDF format) - Reproducibility appendix with complete methods documentation 
 Main Figures (3 figures): - Figure 1: scVI-based integration of PBMCs and extraction of the T-cell compartment - Figure 2: T cells organize along graded functional continua rather than discrete helper clusters - Figure 3: Cytokine and transcription factor signatures validate T-cell functional states 
 Supplementary Figures (12 panels across 2 figure sets): - Figure S3A-G: T-cell functional program scores across the latent space (Naive, Cytotoxic, Th1, Th2, Th17, Tfh, Treg) - Figure S4A-E: Expression of lineage-specific marker genes (IFNG, GZMB, GNLY, FOXP3, CXCR5) 
 Supplementary Tables (5 tables): - Table S1: Mapping of analytical steps to pipeline scripts - Table S2A: Mean functional program scores by Leiden cluster (resolution 0.5) - Table S2B: Mean functional program scores by Leiden cluster (resolution 0.8) - Table S3A: Absolute counts of T-cell functional states per sample - Table S3B: Fractional composition of T-cell functional states per sample 
  KEY FINDINGS 
 This work identifies distinct T cell functional states in systemic sclerosis peripheral blood using single-cell RNA sequencing analysis of 578 PBMC samples (14,617 T cells analyzed). Key findings include: 
 1. T cells exist on graded transcriptional continua rather than discrete clusters in peripheral blood 
 2. Seven distinct functional states identified: Naive, Cytotoxic, Th1, Th2, Th17, Tfh, and Treg 
 3. Th2 and Th17 states produce pro-fibrotic cytokines (IL-4, IL-13, IL-17A) that activate fibroblasts 
 4. Mechanistic bridge between immune activation and stromal fibrosis in systemic sclerosis pathogenesis 
  METHODS SUMMARY 
 Dataset: GEO accession GSE195452 (publicly available) Samples: 578 systemic sclerosis PBMC samples Cells analyzed: 67,592 PBMCs total; 14,617 T cells Batch correction: scVI (Single-cell Variational Inference) T cell extraction: Marker-based identification (CD3D, CD3E, TRAC) Functional state inference: Program-based scoring approach (not traditional clustering) Validation: Leiden clustering at multiple resolutions 
  RELATED RESOURCES 
 Analysis code: https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.18644684 
 Input data: GEO accession GSE195452 (https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/geo/query/acc.cgi?acc=GSE195452) 
 Published paper: Will be added upon publication 
  REPRODUCIBILITY 
 All analyses use fixed random seeds and are fully reproducible. Complete analysis code with documentation is available at the code archive linked above. 
  RELATED WORK 
 This work is part of a comprehensive research program examining the immune-fibrosis pathway in systemic sclerosis: 
 Paper 1: Single-cell transcriptomic analysis identifies drug-reversible fibroblast activation states in systemic sclerosis (Submitted to Frontiers in Medicine, Manuscript ID: 1801050) 
 Paper 2: Single-cell signatures of EBV immune scarring in B-cells (Submitted to PLoS One, Manuscript Number: PONE-D-26-06471) 
 Paper 3: T cell functional states in systemic sclerosis peripheral blood (This work) 
  CITATION 
 Ritschel, G.W. (2026). Single-cell transcriptomic analysis reveals program-based T cell functional states in systemic sclerosis peripheral blood. Frontiers in Immunology. Submitted. 
  LICENSE 
 This work is licensed under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0), consistent with Frontiers in Immunology's open access publishing policy. 
  CONTACT 
 Glen W. Ritschel Email: glen.ritschel@gmail.com
